message: Metformin is contraindicated in significant renal impairment (eGFR <30). Risk of lactic acidosis.
Metformin is contraindicated in renal failure. Risk of lactic acidosis.
Metformin is contraindicated in severe hepatic impairment. Risk of lactic acidosis.
Metformin is contraindicated in severe hepatic impairment.
ACE inhibitors are contraindicated in pregnancy. Risk of fetal renal damage and oligohydramnios.
ACE inhibitors are contraindicated in pregnancy.
ARBs are contraindicated in pregnancy. Risk of fetal renal damage.
ARBs are contraindicated in pregnancy.
Warfarin is contraindicated in pregnancy (especially first trimester). Risk of fetal warfarin syndrome.
Methotrexate is absolutely contraindicated in pregnancy. Teratogenic.
Isotretinoin is absolutely contraindicated in pregnancy. Highly teratogenic.
Tetracyclines are contraindicated in pregnancy. Risk of dental staining and bone growth inhibition.
Fluoroquinolones should be avoided in pregnancy. Risk of cartilage damage.
Fluoroquinolones should be avoided in pregnancy.
Non-selective beta-blockers are contraindicated in asthma. Risk of bronchospasm.
Propranolol is contraindicated in asthma. Risk of severe bronchospasm.
Aspirin may trigger bronchospasm in aspirin-sensitive asthma patients.
Aspirin is contraindicated in active peptic ulcer disease. High GI bleeding risk.
NSAIDs are contraindicated in active peptic ulcer disease.
NSAIDs are contraindicated in active peptic ulcer disease.
Aspirin is contraindicated in active GI bleeding.
Clopidogrel increases bleeding risk in active GI bleeding.
Beta-blockers are contraindicated in second or third degree heart block.
Beta-blockers are contraindicated in second or third degree heart block.
Verapamil is contraindicated in heart failure with reduced ejection fraction.
Thiazide diuretics can precipitate gout attacks by raising uric acid levels.
Loop diuretics can increase uric acid levels and precipitate gout.
Macrolides may exacerbate myasthenia gravis. Use with extreme caution.
Fluoroquinolones may exacerbate myasthenia gravis.
Dapsone is contraindicated in G6PD deficiency. Risk of severe hemolytic anemia.
Primaquine is contraindicated in G6PD deficiency.
Corticosteroids can significantly increase blood glucose. Monitor closely in diabetic patients.
Thiazides may worsen glycemic control. Monitor blood glucose.
Tramadol lowers seizure threshold. Use with caution in epilepsy.
Fluoroquinolones may lower seizure threshold.
Amiodarone can worsen hypothyroidism due to high iodine content.
Azithromycin prolongs QT interval. Avoid in patients with known QT prolongation.
Domperidone prolongs QT interval. Contraindicated in QT prolongation.
Escitalopram may prolong QT interval, especially at doses >20mg.
